seoTitle: Connect Qdrant to n8n for Vector Search Automation
metaDescription: Learn how to connect a Qdrant vector database to n8n, upsert embeddings, and run similarity searches inside your workflows.
intro: Qdrant is a vector database commonly used for semantic search and retrieval-augmented generation, and connecting it to n8n lets you upsert embeddings or run similarity searches as part of an automated pipeline. This guide covers setting up the connection, structuring your vector payloads, and piping results into downstream AI nodes.

steps.heading: Open a new workflow canvas
steps.body: Start with a fresh, empty workflow in your n8n workspace.

steps.heading: Add the Qdrant node
steps.body: Search for and deploy the Qdrant node or vector store component onto your canvas.

steps.heading: Configure your credentials
steps.body: Enter your Qdrant cluster URL and secret API token in the credential submenu.

steps.heading: Choose your operation
steps.body: Decide whether you want to upsert new documents or run a semantic similarity search query.

steps.heading: Set the collection and map your data
steps.body: Define the target collection name and map incoming JSON payloads, including document chunks and metadata.

steps.heading: Connect and test
steps.body: Sequence the node with the rest of your workflow and run a test to confirm data is stored or retrieved correctly.

faq.question: Do I need to already have a Qdrant instance running?
faq.answer: Yes, this integration connects to an existing Qdrant cluster, whether self-hosted or cloud-managed — n8n doesn't provision the database for you.

faq.question: What can I use this integration for?
faq.answer: It's commonly used to build retrieval-augmented generation pipelines, semantic search tools, or AI knowledge repositories.

faq.question: Can I pipe search results into an AI agent node?
faq.answer: Yes, retrieved vector matches can be piped into downstream AI agent nodes or external language models within the same workflow.

faq.question: What data do I need to upsert into a collection?
faq.answer: You'll typically map data points, document chunks, and associated metadata properties as the payload for each vector entry.
